title: Extra Wide Media Base (Modular Playroom Storage Wall)
summary: A low, wide media console with open cubbies, part of a modular storage wall. Deep 21" plywood carcass on a base-moulding foot.
description:
The base unit of a modular playroom storage wall: a low, 52"-wide media console with open cubbies flanking a center bay, built from a deep 21"-wide plywood carcass and dressed with base moulding and nosing so it reads as furniture rather than a plywood box. It's designed to sit under upper cabinets or a hutch in the modular system, but works fine on its own as a TV base or toy storage.

Everything is pocket-holed. Rip your sheet into 21"-wide strips (a table saw is the right tool for a clean, straight rip on cabinet plywood), crosscut to length, and if you're staining rather than painting, iron edge banding onto the exposed front plies. Assembly goes best upside down — build the shelf-and-divider assembly, attach it to the underside of the top, then bring the sides and bottom in.

The two finishing touches that sell it: a square, glued-and-nailed 1/4" back that keeps the whole carcass from racking, and the base moulding plus nosing wrapping the bottom for that built-in look. Cut those to fit rather than to the drawing, since the finished carcass sets the real length.

tools.note: Ripping the sheet into clean 21" strips.

steps.title: Build the center shelf-and-divider assembly
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the 23" center shelf, three per 21" side, on the bottom. Drill three 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the vertical divider pieces for attaching to the top. Assemble the shelf and dividers.

steps.title: Attach the assembly to the top
steps.body: Attach the shelf-and-divider assembly to the underside of the top with 1-1/4" pocket screws, measuring carefully for a 13-3/4" space at each end. This is best done upside down.

steps.title: Add the sides
steps.body: Drill three 3/4" pocket holes on the top edges of each side. Tack them in place with brad nails first so they don't slip, then screw together with 1-1/4" pocket screws.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on each end of the bottom shelf and attach it to the sides with 1-1/4" pocket screws. Nail and screw it to the vertical dividers as well.

steps.title: Add the cubby trim
steps.body: Use 1-1/4" brad nails to attach the 1x2 trim pieces to the insides of the cubbies.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Carefully cut the back piece dead square. Apply glue to the back edges of the cabinet and attach the 1/4" back with 3/4" brad nails, keeping all outside edges perfectly aligned — the back is what squares the carcass.

steps.title: Add moulding and finish
steps.body: Measure and cut the base moulding and nosing to fit, and attach with glue and brad nails for the built-in look. Fill, sand, and finish — paint or stain (edge-band first if staining).

images.alt: Low wide media console with open cubbies and base moulding
